description_long: This object is a stylized representation of a coconut palm tree, featuring a segmented, polygonal brown trunk that tapers as it rises. The trunk is composed of stacked, angular sections, each with visible edges and a slightly irregular shape, giving it a blocky, low-poly appearance. At the top, a crown of large, elongated green fronds radiates outward, each leaf displaying prominent white vein markings and a slightly jagged edge. The leaves are arranged in a circular pattern, drooping gracefully to suggest the weight and flexibility typical of palm fronds. The overall coloration is naturalistic, with a gradient of browns on the trunk and varying shades of green on the leaves. No coconuts or fruit clusters are visible among the fronds, and the object is isolated against a plain background.
description: A stylized coconut palm tree with a segmented brown trunk and a crown of large, veined green fronds. The tree has a blocky, low-poly look and no visible coconuts.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full spread of the palm fronds and the segmented trunk, with the leaves fanning out symmetrically.
description_view_1: This angled view highlights the overlapping fronds and the trunk's polygonal segments, with some leaves partially obscuring others.
description_view_2: Side view emphasizes the vertical alignment of the trunk and the downward droop of the fronds, with the leaf veins clearly visible.
description_view_3: Another angled view showing the fronds from a different perspective, with the trunk's blocky structure and leaf arrangement apparent.
attribute_husk_pattern: No coconut husk is visible; the trunk has a faceted, blocky texture with angular segments, while the fronds display linear white vein markings along their length.
attribute_proximity_to_palm_trees: The object itself is a palm tree; no separate coconuts or additional palm trees are visible.
